_schemaDefinition.name: clean display name from Figma page
_schemaDefinition.figma: nodeId (primary component set), fileKey, link, netlifyFallback
_schemaDefinition.primaryComponent: name of the component set selected as primary for this family
_schemaDefinition.subComponents: all component sets in this family — internal: true flags sub-slots
_schemaDefinition.tokens: brand tokens this component uses (hand-authored or from tokens.json)
_schemaDefinition.dos: hand-authored do list — preserved across re-exports
_schemaDefinition.donts: hand-authored dont list — preserved across re-exports

figmaPush.defaultVariant: auto-derived from props; override manually if needed
figmaPush.requiredProps: props that must be explicitly set before commit

components.name: .Component Status:  Done, 🟡 Inprogress,🔵 Marked for Review , 🔴 Dependency
Accordion
components.description:
Expandable/collapsible sections for FAQs, menus, and content organization.

Keywords: toggle, expand, collapse, disclosure
dos: Use Expanded=True/False to toggle state — never show/hide layers manually
Place all expanded content inside the .Content sub-component
Use for progressive disclosure — secondary details not all users need
donts: Do not use 'Collapsed' or 'Expanded' as the prop value — only 'True' or 'False'
Do not nest accordions more than one level deep
Do not use an Accordion for navigation — use a Drawer or List Item instead

components.name: Action Card
components.description:
A tappable card that presents a primary action or navigation target, combining an icon, title, and description to clearly communicate the destination or outcome. Use for high-level app navigation, feature selection, or as a prominent entry point to a multi-step flow, such as booking a flight, managing a profile, or accessing a service.

Keywords: action card, feature card, navigation card, promo card, call-to-action card, button card, tile, action tile, card button, link card, selection card, promo tile

components.name: Action Footer
components.description:
A bottom-attached panel that displays a summary of costs and actions, often used in checkout flows to confirm selections and proceed to payment. It can expand to show a detailed breakdown and includes configurable call-to-action buttons, a handle bar for dragging, and an optional home indicator for mobile interfaces.

Keywords: action footer, bottom sheet, checkout footer, summary panel, sticky footer, payment footer, cart summary, bottom bar, action bar, expandable panel, modal sheet, bottom navigation

components.name: Alert

content.name: Description
content.defaultValue: Primary message that explains the banner's purpose. This text should be clear an

content.name: Arabic Description
content.defaultValue: الرسالة الأساسية التي تشرح غرض الشريط. يجب أن يكون هذا النص واضحًا وموجزًا، عادة

dos: Always include a Description explaining what happened and what to do next
Use Type=Error for validation failures and failed API calls
Use Type=Success after a positive action completes (booking confirmed, saved)
Use Type=Warning for non-blocking issues the user should be aware of
Set Show Close Button=False for permanent/persistent alerts
donts: Do not write vague alert messages ('Something went wrong') — be specific
Do not use Type=Error for warnings — respect the semantic hierarchy
Do not use Alert for navigation — use an action sheet or a link
Do not stack more than 2 alerts without prioritising which is more urgent
Do not omit Description when Title is present

components.name: App Tutorial
components.description:
A modal dialog that interrupts the user to announce important information, such as a new feature, tutorial, or system update, requiring acknowledgment before continuing. Use for onboarding flows, feature announcements, or critical updates where user attention is mandatory.

Keywords: announcement modal, dialog, popup, overlay, alert dialog, feature announcement, tutorial modal, onboarding, interstitial, lightbox, modal window

content.name: Description
content.defaultValue: This description explains the feature clearly and concisely. Please ensure it do

content.name: Description (Ar)
content.defaultValue: توضح هذه الوصف الميزة بوضوح وإيجاز. يرجى التأكد من عدم تجاوزها ثلاث سطور. اجعل ر

components.name: Avatar
components.description:
A visual representation of a user, typically using an image, initials, or a placeholder.
Keywords: avatar, profile, user, picture, icon, initials, status, tier

components.name: Badge
components.description:
A compact, non-interactive indicator that visually communicates a user's loyalty status or membership tier, often using color, text, and iconography. Use in user profiles, account dashboards, or next to avatars to quickly convey status and unlock visual recognition.
Keywords: loyalty badge, status badge, membership badge, tag, chip, lozenge, pill, indicator, label, status indicator, tier badge

components.name: Booking Widget
components.description:
A card that displays a single flight segment within a multi-city booking, showing departure and destination cities, airport codes, and dates, with a close button for removal. Use in multi-step flight booking flows to let travellers review, edit, or delete individual legs of their itinerary.

Keywords: multi-city card, flight segment, itinerary card, booking step, leg card, trip builder, flight summary, booking widget, travel, journey

components.name: Bottom Navigation Bar
components.description:
Overview:
This component is a navigation tab bar designed for an airline app, featuring five key sections: Home, Book, Stopover, My Trips, and Profile. Each tab is represented by an icon and label, with the active tab highlighted for user clarity. This design enhances user experience by providing quick access to essential features, following best practices for intuitive navigation.

Keywords:
tab bar, bottom navigation, navbar, navigation bar, mobile tabs, app navigation, UI component, menu bar, tab navigation
dos: Use ic_nav_menu_ icons at 32px for all tab icons
Maximum 5 tabs — never exceed
Always include Home Indicator below in full-screen iPhone frames
Set Active Tab variant — do not change icon colour manually
donts: Do not use ic_nav_ (24px) icons — use ic_nav_menu_ (32px) for this component
Do not add more than 5 tabs
Do not use Web Super Nav or Footer as a replacement on App
Do not custom-build a tab bar — always use this library component

components.name: Bullet List
components.description:
A single item in a bulleted or checklist, containing a symbol and descriptive text.
Keywords: bullet, list, item, checklist, point, symbol, description, text, unordered list

content.name: Label
content.defaultValue: Bullet point description text goes here....

components.name: Buttons
components.description:
Button Component
A versatile button component that supports various states, styles, and sizes to accommodate different user interface needs.

Overview
Buttons allow users to trigger actions or events with a single click. They communicate clickable elements of the interface and can contain text labels and/or icons.

Component Variations

Type Variants
Primary (Solid background)
Secondary (Outlined)
White - Outlined
White - Filled

States
Default
Hover
Pressed/Active
Focused
Disabled
Loading

Sizes
Medium (Default)
Large (Extended)


Keywords: button, cta, call to action, primary, secondary, outlined, filled, icon button, toggle, click, trigger, action, submit, form, interactive
dos: Use Type=Primary for the single most important CTA on a screen section
Use State=Loading when the button action is processing — prevents double-tap
Always set a Label prop value — never icon-only without accessible context
Use Size=Small in dense layouts; Size=Large for hero CTAs
Set Language=Arabic when the button is in an Arabic-language context
donts: Do not place more than one Primary button in the same screen section
Do not use State=Hover — App uses Pressed instead (Hover is Web/desktop only)
Do not resize the button below 44px height — WCAG 2.1 touch target minimum
Do not hardcode button colour — use tokens through the component variant
Do not use a button for navigation within a list — use a List Item with arrow

components.name: Carousel Arrows
components.description:
Horizontal scrolling UI for images, cards, or promotions.

Keywords: slider, gallery, swipe, media

components.name: Chips
components.description:
A compact element representing an input, attribute, or action, often used for selected filters or tags.

Keywords:
tag, pill, token, filter tag, label chip, selection pill, badge

content.name: Title
content.defaultValue: Title show here, min 1 line, max 2 lines

content.name: Description
content.defaultValue: Body text and description will show here, min 1 line, max 3 lines

components.name: Controls
components.description:
A control that allows users to toggle between selected, unselected, and indeterminate states, commonly used in forms, lists, and data tables to enable multiple selection or acknowledge terms. Use when users need to make binary choices, select multiple items from a set, or indicate a mixed state for parent-child relationships.
Keywords: checkbox, check box, tick box, selection control, toggle, form field, input, indeterminate, tri-state, Material Checkbox, HIG Checkbox, Fluent Checkbox, Polaris Choice List, Carbon Checkbox, Switch alternative

content.name: Title
content.defaultValue: Destinations around the world for you to discover.

components.name: Divider
components.description:
A thin line or rule used to visually separate content, sections, or list items within a layout to improve readability and information hierarchy. Use between cards, form fields, menu items, or any adjacent elements that need clear visual distinction without adding excess space.

Keywords: divider, separator, rule, line, hairline, border, section divider, list divider, horizontal rule, hr

components.name: Drawers
components.description:
A sliding panel that appears from the edge of the screen to reveal supplementary content, actions, or navigation. Use for mobile-first interfaces to present contextual information, settings, or multi-step workflows without navigating away from the main view.
Keywords: drawer, side panel, sheet, bottom sheet, modal sheet, overlay, sidebar, slide-out panel, navigation drawer, action sheet

components.name: Dropdown
components.description:
A form field that reveals a list of selectable options when clicked, allowing users to choose one item from a predefined set. Use in forms, filters, and settings where space is limited and the full list should remain hidden until needed.

Keywords: dropdown, select, combobox, picker, menu button, selection list, form field, input, choice

content.name: Description
content.defaultValue: Explore our latest features designed to streamline your workflow.

components.name: Helper Text
components.description:
A small, contextual text block that provides supporting information, validation feedback, or status for a related input or section. Use below form fields, in dialogs, or alongside controls to clarify requirements, confirm success, or explain errors without interrupting the user.

Keywords: helper text, supporting text, caption, hint text, validation message, form text, status text, input description, assistive text, error message, success message

components.name: Home Indicator
components.description:
A thin, system-level visual bar that indicates the presence of a software home button or gesture area on modern touchscreen devices. Use at the bottom of full-screen mobile app views to maintain platform familiarity and signal the edge of the interactive screen area.

Keywords: home indicator, home bar, gesture bar, navigation indicator, system bar, iPhone bar, iPad bar, touch affordance, safe area, bottom indicator

components.name: Image Container
components.description:
A container that displays an icon with a label, typically used to represent a category, feature, or action in a compact, visually scannable format. Use in settings menus, feature lists, or navigation cards where a clear icon-and-text pairing helps users quickly identify options.

Keywords: image container, icon label, feature tile, category card, icon button, pictogram, visual indicator, menu item, navigation item, action tile

components.name: Inline Messages
components.description:
A compact, inline status message that appears within a form or input area to provide contextual feedback, validation, or guidance without interrupting the user flow. Use to show field-specific errors, warnings, success confirmations, or helpful hints directly next to the relevant input.

Keywords: inline message, inline alert, field message, validation message, helper text, status text, form feedback, input hint, error text, Material Design helper text, Fluent field message, Polaris inline error

components.name: Input Field
components.description:
A form field that allows users to enter and edit text, with visual states for interaction, validation, and helper text. Use in forms, search bars, settings, and any interface where user input is required, such as entering passenger details, search queries, or payment information.

Keywords: input field, text field, form field, text input, input box, search field, Material Design TextField, Fluent TextBox, Apple HIG Text Field, form control, data entry
dos: Always pair Show Error Message=True with State=Error — both must be set together
Use Helper Text to guide formatting (e.g. 'Enter 6-digit code')
Use State=Read Only for pre-filled values the user cannot change
Use State=Disabled when the field is not applicable in the current form state
Use Leading Icon to reinforce input type (ic_nav_search for search fields)
donts: Do not show Error Message without also setting State=Error
Do not confuse Read Only (visible, uneditable) with Disabled (unavailable)
Do not use Text Area for short single-line inputs — use Input Field
Do not hardcode placeholder colour — use --f-brand-color-text-subtle via the variant
Do not place more than one trailing icon unless the component explicitly supports it

components.name: Keyboard
components.description:
A software keyboard component that displays alphabetic keys, suggestions, and system buttons (space, shift, return) for text input on touchscreen devices. Use in mobile apps and web forms where a native on-screen keyboard is required, such as search fields, messaging, or data entry.

Keywords: keyboard, soft keyboard, virtual keyboard, on-screen keyboard, input method, text input, keypad, alphabetic, iOS keyboard, suggestion bar, predictive text

components.name: Link
components.description:
A text-based interactive control that navigates the user to another page, view, or section, or triggers a secondary action, with visual states for hover, press, and focus. Use inline within paragraphs, in navigation menus, or as a standalone call-to-action where a button would be too prominent.

Keywords: link, anchor, hyperlink, text link, inline link, navigation, action, button link, call-to-action, CTA, Material, HIG, Fluent, Polaris

components.name: Membership Progress Bar
components.description:
A horizontal bar that visually represents a user's progress towards the next loyalty tier, using a gradient fill to indicate the current tier and distance traveled. Use in account dashboards, loyalty program pages, or onboarding flows to motivate continued engagement.

Keywords: membership progress bar, loyalty progress, tier indicator, status bar, progress indicator, level bar, achievement tracker, linear progress, determinate progress, loyalty program, gamification

components.name: Membership Progress Circles
components.description:
A circular progress indicator that visually represents a user's progress towards the next loyalty tier, using a segmented or filled arc to show status. Use in account dashboards, loyalty program hubs, or profile cards to motivate engagement by showing how close a member is to achieving Silver, Gold, or Platinum status.

Keywords: membership progress circle, loyalty progress, tier indicator, radial progress, circular progress, status ring, achievement tracker, loyalty dashboard, progress bar, gauge, meter

components.name: Modal Alert
components.description:
A modal dialog that interrupts the user to present an important message, request confirmation, or prompt a decision, requiring an explicit action to dismiss. Use for critical alerts, confirmations, or blocking tasks where user attention is mandatory, such as confirming a booking change or acknowledging a system error.

Keywords: modal alert, dialog, modal, alert, confirmation, popup, overlay, prompt, action sheet, banner, message box, blocking dialog

A message should be a short,
complete sentence.

content.defaultValue: يجب أن تكون الرسالة جملة قصيرة وكاملة.

components.name: Number Stepper
components.description:
Incremental number input with plus/minus buttons.

Keywords: input, quantity, counter, increase

components.name: OTP Input
components.description:
A One-Time Password (OTP) input component composed of multiple individual digit fields. Used for verification codes, PINs, and other short numeric inputs.

Keywords: OTP, one-time password, PIN input, verification code, code input, digit field, multi-digit input, security code, authentication input, numeric code, passcode, Material OTP, Fluent PIN input, HIG verification field

components.name: Pagination
components.description: A dot indicator used in pagination components to show current position and available pages. Keywords: pagination, dot, indicator, page, nav, navigation, stepper, progress

components.name: Progress Stepper
components.description:
Multi-step navigation or process indicator.

Keywords: progress, steps, form, wizard

components.name: Status Bar
components.description:
A fixed bar at the top of the iPhone screen that displays system status information like time, battery level, Wi‑Fi, and cellular signal strength. Use to mirror the native iOS status bar in mobile app prototypes, ensuring visual consistency and accurate representation of device state.

Keywords: status bar, iPhone, system bar, top bar, battery indicator, time display, signal strength, Wi‑Fi icon, mobile UI, iOS, device chrome, prototype fidelity

components.name: Tooltip
components.description:
A small pop-up that provides additional context or information when a user hovers over or focuses on an element.

Keywords: tooltip, popover, hint, info, description, help, overlay, balloon

components.name: iOS Notifications
components.description:
A message that appears in a fixed position on screen to inform users of system events, updates, or status changes without interrupting their current task. Use for non-critical alerts like flight status updates, booking confirmations, or promotional messages that should be noticed but don't require immediate action.

Keywords: notification, alert, banner, toast, snackbar, message bar, status message, system alert, inline notification, feedback
